macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Measures Diverge Sharply Due to AI Demand and Iran Supply Shock
items.summary: Core PCE inflation surged to 4.1% annualized in February 2026 from 2.4% in November 2025, widening the gap with subdued core CPI, driven by AI-related microchip demand colliding with Iran conflict supply disruptions, complicating Fed rate cut outlook amid stagflation risks.

items.title: U.S. Business Optimism Declines Amid Renewed Inflation, Global Conflict, and Recession Fears
items.summary: Principal Financial Well Being Index fell to 6.06 in March 2026, down 6% from January, with rising concerns over inflation, global financial stability, recession, and energy price volatility; only 17% of employers see U.S. economy growing.

items.title: Trump Tariffs Inflict Significant Damage on U.S. Economy, Exacerbated by Iran War Oil Spike
items.summary: Moody's Mark Zandi states tariffs have stalled job growth outside healthcare and accelerated inflation to 3% YoY; higher energy prices from U.S.-Israel-Iran war threaten further growth damage and higher inflation.

items.title: Q1 GDP Prices Rise 3.6%, Nominal GDP Up 5.6%; Inflation Trends Mixed Above Fed Target
items.summary: GDP prices increased at 3.6% in Q1 2026, up 3.3% YoY, with recent CPI at 3.0% YoY for both overall and core, exceeding Fed's 2% target; older Fed rate cut and shutdown notes mentioned.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $82,000 to Multi-Month High
items.summary: Bitcoin reached a new multi-month peak at $82,000, facing resistance but holding near that level amid reduced Iran tensions and AI enthusiasm; the total crypto market cap rose by $30 billion to over $2.75 trillion.

items.title: Zcash (ZEC) Surges 40-43% in 24 Hours on Privacy Demand
items.summary: Zcash skyrocketed 40-43% in the past 24 hours, climbing above $600, driven by renewed interest in privacy features and a surge in shielded transactions, outperforming most cryptocurrencies.

items.title: XRP Surges Past $1.45, Adds $1 Billion Market Cap in Minutes
items.summary: XRP rose above $1.45 after 70 days of flat trading, gaining over $1 billion in market cap rapidly due to RLUSD crossing $1 billion supply and tokenized STRC launch on XRP Ledger.

items.title: Circle Shares Jump 20% as US Lawmakers Reach Stablecoin Deal
items.summary: Circle's shares surged 20% following a stablecoin regulatory compromise by lawmakers, though opposed by major banking groups shortly after release.

items.title: MicroStrategy Considers First-Ever Bitcoin Sale for Dividends
items.summary: MicroStrategy's Executive Chairman Michael Saylor announced potential liquidation of some of its 818,334 BTC holdings to fund $1.5 billion in dividends, causing shares to drop 4%.

items.title: Digital Asset Funds See $117.8M Inflow, Erasing Midweek $619M Outflows
items.summary: Crypto funds recorded $117.8 million inflows, mainly into Bitcoin ($192M), breaking a four-day outflow streak, while Ethereum and Solana saw outflows amid steady institutional demand.

items.title: TON Skyrockets 30-65% on Telegram Network Changes
items.summary: TON surged 30-65% following major Telegram-driven network updates that could reshape the project, amid broader altcoin gains.

items.title: a16z Crypto Closes $2.2B Fund for Builders Across Stages
items.summary: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) closed a $2.2 billion fund to support crypto builders at all stages, marking significant venture capital commitment to the ecosystem.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5 Instant
items.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 Instant, featuring enhanced capabilities across image, video, website, game, and agent functionalities. The new model represents the latest iteration in OpenAI's GPT series.

items.title: xAI Launches Grok 4.3
items.summary: xAI has released Grok 4.3, the latest version of its AI model. This release continues xAI's rapid iteration cycle in the competitive LLM landscape.

items.title: GitHub Copilot Switches to Per-Token Pricing
items.summary: Microsoft announced that GitHub Copilot will transition to per-token charging starting June 1st, 2026, which may increase costs for medium to heavy users.

items.title: Meta Hires Four OpenAI Researchers
items.summary: Meta has hired four researchers from OpenAI including Shengjia Zhao, Jiahui Yu, Shuchao Bi, and Hongyu Ren, signaling continued talent competition in the AI industry.

items.title: Microsoft Releases AI Agent Runtime Security Toolkit
items.summary: Microsoft has unveiled an open-source toolkit focused on runtime security to enforce strict governance on enterprise AI agents.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Tekteon Fissureflesh: New Legendary Champion Predicted to be Top 3 in Raid Shadow Legends
items.summary: A new Legendary champion, Tekteon Fissureflesh, is hailed as the most dangerous ever released in Raid Shadow Legends and is expected to dominate the top tiers based on test server footage.

items.title: CS2 Hit by Game-Breaking Grenade Exploit Bug Ruining Competitive Play
items.summary: Counter-Strike 2 servers are plagued by a new grenade exploit bug affecting Premier and competitive matchmaking, leading to bans; Valve has a pending update, amid news of a mobile port announcement, Nuke map removal from pro pools, and declining player counts.

items.title: Neverwinter Module 33 Updates: Arena Ends, Celestial Rings Return, No Gear Nerfs
items.summary: Neverwinter developers announce the end of the arena, return of Celestial Rings, new combat enchants, and reversion of old gear nerfs, with Module 33 expansion details forthcoming.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Electronics Hits $1 Trillion Valuation After Breaking Big Tech Profit Records
items.summary: Samsung Electronics achieved a $1 trillion market valuation, surpassing Apple and Microsoft in quarterly operating profit, fueled by surging demand for memory chips.

items.title: Apple to Pay $250 Million Settlement for Delayed AI-Powered Siri Delivery
items.summary: Apple has agreed to a proposed $250 million settlement in a California class action lawsuit over failing to deliver its AI-powered Siri on time.

items.title: Snap's $400 Million Deal with Perplexity Falls Through
items.summary: The planned $400 million AI search feature integration for Snapchat never fully rolled out, leading to the deal's collapse.

items.title: Xbox Ditches Microsoft's Copilot AI for Consoles and Mobile App
items.summary: Microsoft is removing the Copilot AI assistant from Xbox consoles and its mobile app, scrapping plans for its integration.

items.title: Nuro Approved to Test Driverless Uber Robotaxis on California Roads
items.summary: Nuro and Uber have received approval for fully autonomous robotaxi testing on California public roads, with plans to start later this year.
